phrase: A you game? / Are you up for it? / Are you down?
meaning: Asking if someone is interested or willing. "Are you down for karaoke tonight?"
Politely letting someone go first.
I don't have the patience or interest for that.

phrase: Already ahead of you / Already on it
meaning: I've already started doing that without being asked.
Okay, agreed. Casual confirmation. "Bet" alone works too.
Asking if you can be heard clearly (on a call or video).
You're welcome; don't mention it.
Based on what I've heard; it seems that. "Apparently the meeting got cancelled."
Asking for confirmation; expressing mild disbelief.
Very well-behaved or reliable.

phrase: At the end of the day
meaning: Ultimately; when everything is considered. "At the end of the day, results matter."
The bar had no energy or people.
Boring, unoriginal, lacking personality. "That song is so basic."
I have no idea. "Where did he go?" — "Beats me."
An arrogant or conceited person.
To do a lot of something at once, especially watching shows. "We binge-watched the whole series."
A stupid or careless person.
Good luck (used especially before a performance).
A noisy commotion or fuss.
Something disappointing. "That's a bummer."
Be quiet; stop talking.
Tacky, unoriginal, or embarrassingly uncool.
Describes food that requires a lot of chewing. Also slang for something tough to deal with.
Relax; calm down.
Hurry up; move faster.
Something very easy. "This exam is a cinch."
To quit something suddenly and completely. "She quit smoking cold turkey."
Pardon? Could you repeat that?
Done for; in a hopeless situation. "If we miss this deadline, we're cooked."
A calm, composed person.

phrase: Could care less / Couldn't care less
meaning: I don't care at all. (Note: "couldn't care less" is the correct form.)
Embarrassingly awkward or uncomfortable. "That joke was cringe."
A useless or unproductive person.
I like it; I appreciate it.
Feeling lightheaded or overwhelmed.
Checking if the listener understands.
Don't worry about it; it's not a big deal.
Stay focused; don't daydear.
A word used when you can't remember the name of something. "Hand me that thingamajig."
Cool, excellent. "That's a dope jacket."
Exhausted; completely worn out. "I feel drained when work runs late."
Great personal style; fashionable appearance.
An intellectual; a bookish person.
This has gone too far; stop it now.
To accept the consequences of your actions. "It's time to face the music."
That's reasonable; I accept that point.
To experience lightheadedness or disorientation.
I understand and empathize with you.
Great, awesome. "This pizza is fire."
Completely astonished or shocked.
To show off. "He's always flexing his new shoes."
The feeling of something stuck or scratchy in your throat. (Likely "frog in your throat.")
Talking nonsense; making empty claims. "Don't believe him — he's full of hot air."
Covered in soft hair or fluff.
A person who is enthusiastic about cars or mechanics.
To suddenly cut off contact with someone without explanation. "He ghosted me after our date."
High-five; a celebratory hand slap.
To proceed at the speed that suits me.
To go crazy or get very excited. "The crowd went bananas."
Language that is meaningless or impossible to understand.
That's great for you; congratulations (can be sincere or sarcastic depending on tone).
I understand; message received.
Done with little effort or care. "That was a half-assed attempt."
Irritable because of hunger (hungry + angry). "Sorry I snapped — I was just hangry."
To feel nervous or excited. "I have butterflies before every presentation."
To have a mild runny nose.
He makes big claims but never follows through.
He's very skilled at math (casual, slightly non-standard grammar).
Clearly and openly; no doubt about it. "Hi key obsessed with this song."
It affects me in a unique or unexpected way.
Contact me; reach out. "Hit me up when you're free."
To study. "I need to hit the books tonight."
Having a rough, scratchy voice.
Wait; slow down. "Hold your horses — let me finish."
Someone who gets angry quickly.
A loud, confused noise or fuss.
Very excited or enthusiastic.
I've experienced that; I know what that's like.
I'm so overwhelmed I can't deal with this.
I depend heavily on caffeine to get through the day.

phrase: I could eat tons of [X] and not [Y]
meaning: Used to describe a personal quirk about diet or habits.
Rejecting a label. "I don't consider myself a workaholic, but I do care a lot."
I'm not in the mood for something. "I don't feel like coffee right now."
I'm mentally and physically exhausted.

phrase: I guess it depends on X — X or X
meaning: The answer varies based on circumstances.
I suppose; I think so (with some uncertainty).
Describing a personal physical or mental tendency. "I have a brain where I need silence to focus."

phrase: I have really bad ADHD, if that's even a thing
meaning: Casual, self-aware way of mentioning a condition while acknowledging it's debated.
I was right; I suspected this all along.
I love my romantic partner.
I made a mistake.

phrase: I never do X / I never have less than five coffees
meaning: Emphasizing a consistent personal habit.

phrase: I never expected to be doing [X]
meaning: Expressing surprise at how life turned out.

phrase: I never felt like I was [X]
meaning: Reflecting on self-perception. "I never felt like I was the smartest in the room."

phrase: I never thought I'd end up [doing X]
meaning: Expressing surprise at an unexpected life outcome.
I'm indebted to you for helping me.

phrase: I was never gonna do X, but I ended up doing it because…
meaning: Explaining an unexpected change of plans.
Reflecting on a past limitation. "I wasn't confident enough to speak up."
Something caught me off-prepared.
Expressing a preference. "I'd rather work from home than commute."
Stating a rough frequency. Use round numbers (90%, 95%) in natural speech, not 71%.
No thanks; I'll decline.
I'm listening; go ahead and tell me.

phrase: I'm all in / I'm always either all in or not at all
meaning: I commit fully or not at all; no half-measures.
Nearly at a state or milestone. "I'm almost ready." / "I'm almost 40."
That's so funny I can't handle it.
I'm fine / No thank you.
Count me in; I want to participate.
I'm craving or really wanting something.
I'm already handling it.
I'm not participating.
I'm very tired and exhausted.
I'm so jealous.
I'm in serious trouble; I'm finished.
I don't have the motivation to do it.

phrase: I've been doing X for quite a long time
meaning: Indicating a long-running habit or activity.
Giving a somewhat reluctant or approximate opinion.
Uncertain, unreliable, or not quite right. "The weather looks iffy today."
Concerning this particular matter.
A taunt meaning "I was right" or "you lost."
Questioning whether something actually exists or is valid.
These things occur; don't worry too much about it.

phrase: It is what it is / That's life
meaning: Accepting an unfortunate situation that can't be changed.
It was an extremely difficult or unpleasant experience.
I'll pay for this; it's my responsibility.
This is your fault or your responsibility.
To cut food into thin matchstick strips.
For no particular reason; on a whim.
To die (humorous/informal). "He kicked the bucket at 95."
Roughly; approximately; not exactly.
Let me think about it overnight before deciding.
Take a moment to fully process that.
Let's stop working for today.
Let's finish this up.
Amazing, exciting, excellent. "The party last night was lit."
To spend time aimlessly or move slowly.

phrase: Long story short / In a nutshell
meaning: To summarize briefly.
Quietly; subtly; without drawing attention. "Low key obsessed with this show."
Secretly or quietly; without drawing attention. "I lowkey want to leave early."
A dramatic gesture of confidence after saying something impactful.
Something very relatable; I feel that. "That meme is such a mood."
My mistake; I'm sorry.
No; informal refusal.
I'm doing great; couldn't be better.
It's not a big deal.
An obvious decision that requires no thought.
Take your time; there's no urgency.
No problem; easy; don't worry.
Fairly good (often understated praise).
Not quite; more of a soft no.
That's exactly what I wanted to hear; now we're getting somewhere.
Are you crazy?
Accepting something unfortunate; nothing can be done.
Extremely happy. "She's been on cloud nine since the promotion."
Perfectly done; flawless (somewhat dated slang). "Her eyebrows are on fleek."
Not working properly; out of balance.

phrase: Popular with the ladies / ladies' man
meaning: Someone who is attractive to or liked by women.
To move a vehicle to the side of the road and stop.
Very excited and energized.
Be quiet; stop talking. "Put a sock in it — I'm trying to focus."
Unusual or eccentric in a charming way.
To inform on someone; to tell on them.
To sound familiar; to remind you of something.
To do something confidently and stylishly. "She's rocking that outfit."
Bitter or upset, especially over something minor. "She's still salty about losing."
Me too; I feel the same way.
The same routine as always; nothing has changed.
Ruthless, bold, impressively blunt. "That comeback was savage."

phrase: She is on the ball / She is very together
meaning: She is sharp, organized, and reliable.
To shine with a soft, wavering light.
To support or root for a romantic relationship. "I ship those two together."
Shocked or surprised. "I was shook by the news."
A secondary job or income stream outside of your main work.
To avoid work; to not put in full effort.
To flatter someone in order to gain favor.
To do something exceptionally well. "You really slayed that presentation."
To cuddle up closely in a warm or affectionate way.
A gullible or easily deceived person.
I stopped following; I didn't understand that part.
That's a good idea; let's do that.
To be a huge fan of someone or something. "I stan this album."
To flatter or overly please someone for personal gain.
Very; extremely. "I'm super competitive."
In poor taste; gaudy; socially inappropriate.
Sharp, sour taste.
I totally agree; I know exactly what you mean.
Expressing surprise at the extent of something.
That's disappointing.
That's an entirely different thing.
That's exactly right; that's the one.
That's excellent; that's awesome.

phrase: That's life / It is what it is
meaning: Accepting an unfortunate reality.
I'll take responsibility for that; I'm paying.
That's cool; that's impressive.
That's amazing or very cool.
That's unfortunate; I'm sorry to hear that.
There's no harm in doing X. "There's no detriment to asking for help."
To the fullest extent possible.
Absolutely; I completely agree.
A feeling or atmosphere. "This place has a chill vibe."
Casual greeting meaning "what's up?"
Well done; congratulations.
We're going to improvise without a plan.
To be the one in charge in a relationship.
Any option is fine; go with what functions.
Something that will never happen. "He'll clean his room when pigs fly."
Aware of social and political issues. "He's very woke and informed." (Note: now often used ironically.)
To throw something with force; or an exclamation of excitement. "He yeeted the ball across the room."
Are you ready? Is everything okay?
Both versions are acceptable.

phrase: You can't let X break you
meaning: Don't let something defeat or discourage you.
Do whatever feels right for you; be yourself.
Is that so? (Often ironic or expressing mild surprise.)
The answer is unpleasant or complicated — trust me.

phrase: You got roasted / I got roasted
meaning: You were mocked or teased, usually in a humorous way.
You can do it; I believe in you.

phrase: You gotta keep doing X at 120%
meaning: You must give maximum effort consistently.
You have to acknowledge and admire that.

phrase: You killed it / You slayed it
meaning: You did an outstanding job.
Something you did or said made me very happy.
Are you crazy?
Be quiet; stop talking.
